text: ♪ "If" and "then" are two regular words ♪
♪ that work together exquisitely. ♪
♪ If I can show you by singing this song, ♪
♪ then you'll remember it easily. ♪
♪ I'll give you a tool that starts with a rule ♪
♪ to tell you what the next thing you do ought to be. ♪
♪ Now listen carefully! ♪
♪ If the weather is sunny... ♪
♪ ...then wear a cap. ♪
♪ If you want to go hiking... ♪
♪...then get a map. ♪
♪ If you picture these two words... ♪
♪ ...then it's an easy decision. ♪
♪ If there was ever a time to get down, ♪
♪ then this is one. ♪
♪ If the music is off, then turn it on. ♪
♪ If the music is on, then turn it up. ♪
♪ If the music is up, then hit the floor. ♪
♪ And if you're on the floor, then dance. ♪
♪ "If" and "then" are two regular words ♪
♪ but what a marvelous job they do. ♪
♪ If there's a part with an "if" at the start, ♪
♪ find out if what's in that part is true. ♪
♪ Now wasn't that fun? ♪
♪ You're practically done... ♪
♪...because the "then" part tells you ♪
♪ what you ought to do. ♪
♪ Now you can try it too. ♪
♪ If it's finally snack time... ♪
♪ ...then have some fruit. ♪
♪ If today  is your birthday... ♪
♪ ...then give a toot. ♪ [HORN HONKS]
♪ If you follow my method... ♪
♪ ...then it will give you the know-how. ♪
♪ If there was ever a time to get down, ♪
♪ then it's right now. ♪
♪ If the music is off, then turn it on. ♪
♪ If the music is on, then turn it up. ♪
♪ If the music is up, then hit the floor. ♪
♪ And if you're on the floor, then dance. ♪
♪ If you're on the floor, then dance, dance, dance.♪
♪ Woah, dance now. ♪
♪ If you're on the floor, then dance, dance, dance. ♪
♪ Helpsters! ♪
